sections.subtitle: Current Form
sections.text: Nottingham Forest are in solid form, remaining unbeaten in their last 4 matches with a notable 3-1 win over Brentford. West Ham United, on the other hand, are struggling, having lost their last 3 matches and conceding heavily with no points in the league.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In the last two head-to-head encounters, Nottingham Forest have dominated with wins in both matches (3-0 at home and 2-1 away). Their attacking prowess and West Ham's defensive frailties were evident in these games.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Nottingham Forest rank well in key attacking stats, creating big chances (3rd in league) and maintaining high ball possession (6th). West Ham, at the bottom of the table, have struggled defensively, conceding heavily (8 goals in 2 matches) and failing to secure points.

sections.subtitle: Key Factors
sections.text: Forest's ability to score in crucial periods (late first half) and West Ham's tendency to concede first (5/7 matches) suggest an advantage for the home side. Additionally, West Ham's lack of clean sheets (4 matches) further tilts the balance.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Overall first half performance
halftimeSections.text: The first half at the City Ground ended in a goalless draw between Nottingham Forest and West Ham United. Nottingham Forest showed dominance in possession and territory, frequently entering the final third and penalty area with more intent. However, they failed to convert their control into clear-cut chances, lacking the finishing touch. West Ham United, on the other hand, were more conservative in their approach but managed to create a slightly higher threat in front of goal despite fewer attacking entries. Both teams have shown discipline defensively, with neither conceding significant opportunities.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum analysis
halftimeSections.text: The momentum in the first half swung back and forth with notable shifts between the two sides. Nottingham Forest started with early attacking intent, controlling the game in the opening minutes before West Ham responded with a brief spell of pressure. As the half progressed, Forest regained their footing with sustained periods of dominance, particularly in the middle and latter stages, pushing forward with purpose. West Ham had their moments, especially towards the end of the half, with a few dangerous surges that hinted at their potential to strike. Overall, Forest dictated the tempo for longer stretches, but West Ham's sporadic bursts kept the game balanced.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key team statistics
halftimeSections.text: Nottingham Forest held a slight edge in ball possession and demonstrated greater control in the attacking third, with a higher number of entries and corner kicks. Their ability to win duels, both on the ground and in the air, gave them a physical advantage over West Ham. However, their expected goals were minimal, reflecting a lack of sharpness in the final delivery. West Ham United, while less dominant in possession, created a marginally better threat in front of goal as per their expected goals. They managed to get a shot on target, showing more efficiency with fewer opportunities, though their overall presence in the attacking areas was limited compared to Forest.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Standout players
halftimeSections.text: For Nottingham Forest, Elliot Anderson was a standout performer in midfield, winning multiple tackles and contributing with a key pass, showing energy and creativity. Morgan Gibbs-White also played a pivotal role in the attack, carrying a notable threat in terms of expected goals. On the West Ham side, Mateus Fernandes impressed with his composure in midfield, registering a key pass and contributing defensively with tackles and interceptions. Lucas Paquetá showed glimpses of danger with a shot on target and a significant expected goals contribution, hinting at his potential to unlock the game.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Tactical outlook for second half
halftimeSections.text: Nottingham Forest are likely to continue their high-pressing game, looking to capitalize on their territorial dominance by creating more decisive chances in the penalty area. They may focus on utilizing their wingers and full-backs to stretch West Ham's defense. West Ham United will probably aim to exploit counter-attacking opportunities, relying on quick transitions to catch Forest off guard. They might look to involve Paquetá and Jarrod Bowen more in the final third to turn their limited but dangerous attacks into goals. Both teams could benefit from tactical substitutions to inject fresh legs and ideas into the game.
